steps.title: Identify what “specific” refers to
steps.content: The strongest clue is the phrase “this specific view” — Moss is pointing to a particular scene of the Port of Oakland visible as the train climbs the elevated track. He's singling out one view from all possible views, one that he never tires of.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: ”Specific” most nearly means particular — a specific view is a particular, distinct view that can be identified and cherished. The other three don't fit: “imaginary” contradicts the real scene he's pointing at, “energetic” doesn't describe what a view is, and “correct” has no connection to the context. That matches choice D — D is correct.
